mnemonics: Count & Copy: Compare counts to correctly copy elements into sorted order.
keyPoints: Counts the number of elements smaller than each item to determine its final sorted position.
Not an in-place algorithm; it uses additional arrays for counting and output.
Stable sort: elements with the same value maintain their original relative order.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize the count array to zeros, leading to incorrect counts.
Incorrectly calculating the final positions by not using cumulative counts.
Confusing it with other counting sorts that use frequency counts and prefix sums.
